data_source: Web search (KEYNOTE-522 safety data, ESMO 2024, real-world studies)
extraction_notes: Grade 3+ irAE: 13.0% vs 1.5%. Discontinuation of any drug: 27.6% vs 14.1%. Fatal events: 2 (pneumonitis, autoimmune encephalitis) vs 0. Real-world studies report 12-13.6% grade 3+ irAE, 16-21% ICI discontinuation.
Grade 3-4 AE: 56.7% vs 53.0% (balanced). Serious AE: 22.6% vs 15.6%. Long-term safety consistent with primary results. Most AE driven by chemotherapy, not atezolizumab.
Thyroid dysfunction any grade: 47% in durvalumab arm (higher than placebo). Specific grade 3-4 AE rates not reported in accessible abstracts.

data_source: Web search (Gianni et al 2022 Annals Oncol)
extraction_notes: Treatment-related AE similar between arms. Immune side-effects as expected. Specific grade 3-4 rates require full-text access (not in abstracts).
Grade 3+ AE: 89.2% vs 83.1% (neoadjuvant phase). Serious AE: 34.7% vs 22.8%. Fatal AE: 2 (0.9%) vs 0 in camrelizumab arm.
